model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Previous scheduler change hurt, so revert focus to optimizer dynamics. AdamW often improves short-budget generalization versus Adam by decoupling weight decay with minimal code complexity.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Cosine decay across only 256 steps likely over-anneals too early for this MLP. Reverting to constant LR should recover lost optimization progress within the fixed short budget.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With only 256 training steps and no schedule, a slightly higher learning rate can improve early optimization progress for this MLP while keeping the change minimal and reversible.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Higher LR degraded loss, suggesting overshooting. A slightly lower LR than baseline may improve stability and validation loss in the short fixed-step regime.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Loss improved when reducing LR from 5e-4 to 4e-4, so a small additional decrease may further improve stability and validation performance under the same short training budget.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The LR reduction to 3.5e-4 regressed, so reverting to 4e-4 restores the strongest observed setting before testing further changes.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Learning-rate tuning stabilized at 4e-4, so next low-complexity lever is regularization. A modestly higher AdamW weight decay can improve validation loss under the short fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"A small weight-decay increase produced a slight improvement; continue along the same axis with a modest additional increase to test whether regularization is still under-tuned.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Weight decay at 5e-4 slightly regressed relative to 3e-4, so reverting to 3e-4 restores the best observed regularization setting before further exploration.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.05","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With optimizer and LR near a plateau, introduce light dropout to reduce overfitting without materially changing optimization dynamics or model capacity.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.05","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Dropout caused a large regression in this short-budget setting, so revert to no-dropout to recover the best observed behavior.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_BATCHNORM = True","new":"USE_BATCHNORM = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Test a simpler MLP block without batch normalization; in short fixed-step training, BN noise and extra parameters can hurt optimization, and removing it is a clean architectural ablation.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_BATCHNORM = False","new":"USE_BATCHNORM = True","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Disabling batch normalization caused a major regression, so revert to the prior architecture configuration that supports the best observed loss.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"delete_exact","function":"","old":"","new":"","anchor":"","text":"            torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 5.0)\n","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"For AdamW on this small MLP, gradient clipping may unnecessarily damp updates; removing it is a minimal loop-level change that can improve short-budget convergence.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"insert_after","function":"","old":"","new":"","anchor":"            loss.backward()\n","text":"            torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 5.0)\n","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Removing gradient clipping regressed validation loss, so restore the previous stable training-step behavior.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.8e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Current configuration is stable at a plateau; a small downward LR nudge may improve final validation loss without changing other tuned settings.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.8e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The 3.8e-4 learning rate regressed, so revert to 4e-4 to return to the strongest observed operating point.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"In fixed-step evaluation, increasing batch size raises total examples seen within 256 steps, which can improve validation loss if optimization remains stable.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch size 128 produced a large gain in fixed-step mode; increasing to 256 may further improve validation loss by exposing the model to more unique examples within the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":[]}
